sections.paragraph:
Ἀθηναῖος, α, ον (Hom. et al.; Philo, Joseph.; Ar. ins; Tat.; Mel., HE 4, 26, 10; Ath.) Athenian; subst. ὁ Ἀ. the Athenian Ac 17:21; in dir. address: ἄνδρες Ἀθηναῖοι gentlemen of Athens (Demosth. 8, 35; 18, 27; Diod S 13, 102, 2 al.) vs. 22.—DDD s.v. Athena.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

dir. dir. = direct

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

DDD DDD = Dictionary of Deities and Demons in the Bible—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 24
